title: Senior Pictures Tri-Cities WA | Class of 2027 Photographer
description: Senior pictures in the Tri-Cities that actually look like you. Relaxed golden-hour sessions in Richland, Kennewick and Pasco with wardrobe help and easy posing.

hero.heading: Senior Pictures in the Tri-Cities, WA
hero.intro: Relaxed, confident senior pictures in Richland, Kennewick and Pasco — planned around your style, photographed in golden light, and guided so you never have to wonder what to do with your hands.

sections.heading: This Year Only Happens Once
paragraphs: Senior year moves fast — school, games, applications, lasts of everything. Your senior pictures are the one place where all of it slows down for an evening: who you are right now, before everything changes.

I've photographed seniors across the Tri-Cities for two decades, and here's what I've learned: the best senior pictures don't come from copying someone else's Pinterest board. They come from celebrating your personality, your style, and your story. That's what we'll capture.

sections.heading: "I'm Not Photogenic" — Yes, You Are
paragraphs: Almost every senior tells me some version of this before their session. And then there's my favorite moment of every shoot: turning the camera around and showing them the back of it. Watching a shy or unsure teenager's face light up when they see just how beautiful they are — that never gets old. Not after hundreds of sessions.

You don't need to know how to pose. That's my job. I'll guide you with simple prompts the whole way — walk here, look there, take a deep breath in through your nose and out through your mouth — and somewhere between "get closer!" and "just one more!" (it's basically never just one more), you'll forget the camera is there.

items.heading: Plan (outfits, locations, the vibe)
paragraphs: Before your session, we'll talk through outfits together — what shows your style, what photographs beautifully, and what you can actually move in. Then we'll pick locations around the Tri-Cities that match your vibe: riverside, open fields, urban, or somewhere that's already yours.

items.heading: Shoot (golden hour, zero awkwardness)
paragraphs: We meet at golden hour and keep it moving — multiple outfits, multiple spots if we've planned them, music if you want it. Bring a friend, bring your mom, bring your dog. Whatever helps you feel like you.

items.heading: Reveal (the part parents cry at)
paragraphs: When your gallery is ready, you'll see yourself the way I see you. I've sat with parents seeing their senior's images for the first time — eyes filling up because their "baby" has grown up, or because their child was truly captured for who they are. It happens almost every time.

sections.heading: For Parents
paragraphs: I know these photos are for both of you — for the announcement, the wall, and the season you're walking through as a family. My promise to you: your senior will be guided, safe and comfortable the entire session, and the photographs will show the young adult you know — not a stiff, over-posed version of them. Many of my senior families have been with me since those seniors were little.

items.heading: When should I take my senior pictures?
paragraphs: Most Tri-Cities seniors shoot the summer before senior year or early fall, ahead of yearbook deadlines. Spring of junior year works beautifully too.

links.label: Full timeline: When to take senior pictures

items.heading: How many outfits can I bring?
paragraphs: We'll plan outfits together during your consultation — enough for variety without rushing the session.

items.heading: Can I bring someone with me?
paragraphs: Please do. A parent, a best friend, even your dog — whatever helps you relax and have fun.

items.heading: Where do senior sessions happen?
paragraphs: Outdoors, in golden light, anywhere in the Tri-Cities — Richland, Kennewick, Pasco or the fields and river spots in between. We choose together.

finalCta.heading: Class of 2027 — Let's Plan Yours
paragraphs: Senior dates fill fastest of anything I photograph — summer and fall book out first. Tell me about yourself and let's find your light.
